questions.question: Briefly explain why a decompiler cannot perfectly reconstruct the original source code of a program.
expectedPoints: Loss of information during compilation (e.g., variable names, comments, data structures)
Compiler optimizations that rearrange and simplify code logic
The presence of obfuscation techniques designed to thwart analysis

questions.question: Describe the process of decompilation, from binary to high-level code. Include the purpose of creating an Intermediate Representation (IR).
expectedPoints: Initial loading of the binary and disassembly into assembly instructions
Lifting of assembly code into a processor-agnostic Intermediate Representation (IR)
Purpose of IR: To facilitate easier program analysis (control flow, data flow)
Key analysis steps: Control flow graph construction, data flow analysis, type propagation
Final generation of high-level code (e.g., C) based on the analyzed IR
